channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Fully Funded & Self-Funded PhD Opportunities 2026: College of Business and Social Sciences Programmes at Aston University, Birmingham
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
If you are looking to advance your academic journey and build a strong research career, the PhD opportunities available through the College of Business and Social Sciences listed on FindAPhD present an excellent pathway. These programs are designed for ambitious individuals who want to contribute to global knowledge in areas such as business, economics, management, […]

The post Fully Funded & Self-Funded PhD Opportunities 2026: College of Business and Social Sciences Programmes at Aston University, Birmingham appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are looking to advance your academic journey and build a strong research career, the PhD opportunities available through the College of Business and Social Sciences listed on FindAPhD present an excellent pathway. These programs are designed for ambitious individuals who want to contribute to global knowledge in areas such as business, economics, management, and social sciences.

The platform provides access to multiple funded and self-funded PhD programmes, allowing students worldwide to explore diverse research opportunities across leading universities.

Why Choose a PhD in Business and Social Sciences?

PhD programmes in this field are highly valuable because they:

  • Develop advanced research and analytical skills
  • Open doors to academic, policy, and industry careers
  • Allow candidates to specialize in high-impact areas
  • Provide opportunities to publish in top academic journals
  • Offer exposure to international research networks

According to available listings, these programmes are structured to equip students with both theoretical knowledge and practical research expertise.

Fields of Study Available

The College of Business and Social Sciences covers a wide range of disciplines, including:

Business & Management

  • Accounting
  • Finance
  • Marketing
  • Operations Management
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Corporate Governance

Economics

  • Microeconomics
  • Macroeconomics
  • Econometrics
  • Development Economics

Social Sciences

  • Political Science
  • Sociology
  • Psychology
  • Social Data Science

These fields allow candidates to explore interdisciplinary research topics that address real-world challenges.

Types of PhD Opportunities

The listings typically include:

  1. Funded PhD Programmes
    • Cover tuition fees
    • Provide stipends for living expenses
    • May include research funding
  2. Self-Funded PhDs
    • Students finance their own studies
    • Flexible entry throughout the year
  3. Research Projects
    • Focused topics with assigned supervisors
    • Often part of larger funded research initiatives
  4. Research Programmes
    • Broader academic pathways
    • Allow students to define their research direction

Some opportunities are open to international students worldwide, depending on funding and institutional requirements.

Key Benefits of These PhD Programmes

Students who enroll in these programmes can expect:

  • Access to world-class faculty and supervisors
  • Opportunities for international collaboration
  • Use of modern research facilities and databases
  • Participation in academic conferences and workshops
  • Career pathways in:
    • Academia (lecturer, professor)
    • Government and policy institutions
    • Private sector and consulting

Many graduates go on to secure positions in top universities and global organizations.

Application Process

While requirements vary by university and programme, the general process includes:

  1. Search for a suitable PhD programme
  2. Review eligibility criteria
  3. Prepare required documents, such as:
    • Academic transcripts
    • Research proposal
    • CV/Resume
    • Recommendation letters
  4. Contact potential supervisors (if required)
  5. Submit an online application

Applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure all documentation meets the institution’s standards.

Important Deadlines

Deadlines vary depending on the specific PhD programme. However:

  • Some projects have fixed deadlines (e.g., May–July 2026)
  • Others accept applications year-round

It is important to check each listing carefully to avoid missing out on opportunities.

Who Should Apply?

These PhD opportunities are ideal for:

  • Graduates with a strong academic background
  • Individuals passionate about research and innovation
  • Professionals seeking career advancement in academia or industry
  • Candidates with quantitative and analytical skills

Tips for a Successful Application

To improve your chances of selection:

  • Write a clear and compelling research proposal
  • Align your interests with the supervisor’s expertise
  • Highlight your academic achievements and experience
  • Demonstrate impact and relevance of your research

Final Thoughts

PhD programmes in the College of Business and Social Sciences offer a powerful opportunity to shape your academic and professional future. Whether you are interested in business innovation, economic policy, or social transformation, these programmes provide the tools and environment needed to succeed.

By leveraging platforms like FindAPhD, you can discover opportunities that match your goals and take the next step toward becoming a global researcher.

For more information about this opportunity, click here.

Discover more global Scholarship and Doctoral opportunities on OFY.

The post Fully Funded & Self-Funded PhD Opportunities 2026: College of Business and Social Sciences Programmes at Aston University, Birmingham appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: IFE Call for Proposals 2026: Funding Opportunity for Job-Creating Projects in Côte d’Ivoire, Egypt, and Morocco
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The Facility Investing for Employment (IFE), implemented by KfW Development Bank on behalf of the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ), has officially launched its 2026 Call for Proposals. This initiative is part of the Special Initiative “Decent Work for a Just Transition”, aimed at boosting sustainable employment in Africa. The program […]

The post IFE Call for Proposals 2026: Funding Opportunity for Job-Creating Projects in Côte d’Ivoire, Egypt, and Morocco appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Facility Investing for Employment (IFE), implemented by KfW Development Bank on behalf of the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ), has officially launched its 2026 Call for Proposals. This initiative is part of the Special Initiative “Decent Work for a Just Transition”, aimed at boosting sustainable employment in Africa.

The program is designed to support private sector projects that create meaningful and sustainable jobs, while also enhancing workforce skills and improving working conditions. It focuses on long-term impact, ensuring that funded projects contribute to inclusive economic growth across partner countries.

The application window is open from 15 April to 30 June 2026 (17:00 CEST).

About the Facility Investing for Employment (IFE)

The IFE is a grant-based funding mechanism focused on:

  • Promoting job creation in the private sector
  • Supporting economically sustainable projects
  • Improving working conditions and income levels
  • Enhancing skills development and training opportunities

Projects are evaluated not just on job creation but also on their broader social and economic impact, ensuring that benefits extend beyond immediate employment outcomes.

.

Key Objectives of the IFE Program

Projects submitted under this call should aim to:

  • Create new jobs
  • Improve existing employment conditions
  • Support workforce development through training
  • Promote inclusive growth, especially for:
    • Women
    • Youth
  • Align with sustainable development goals and just transition principles

Funding Components

1. Job Creation (Mandatory)

All applicants must submit projects that:

  • Are financially and operationally sustainable
  • Have not yet started
  • Demonstrate clear job creation potential
  • Include co-financing from the applicant

Projects must fall within one of IFE’s approved investment categories.

2. Training Measures (Optional – Egypt & Morocco Only)

This component supports:

  • Skills development for local labor markets
  • Training with potential pathways to employment in Germany

Eligible Sectors:

  • Construction and related industries
  • Metal and electrical industries
  • Education and social services
  • Food processing
  • Transport and logistics
  • IT sector
  • Hospitality industry
  • Health sector (Egypt only)

Supported Activities:

  • Building or upgrading training facilities
  • Technical and vocational training
  • Language training programs
  • Workforce upskilling aligned with market needs

Evaluation Criteria

Projects will be assessed based on:

  • Number of new jobs created
  • Cost per job
  • Improvement in working conditions
  • Number of beneficiaries trained
  • Bonus points for:
    • Women and youth employment
    • Contribution to gender equality
    • Alignment with sustainability goals
    • Collaboration with German development projects

Eligibility Requirements

Individual Applicants:

  • Must be a registered legal entity
  • Located in Côte d’Ivoire, Egypt, or Morocco
  • Operational for at least 3 years

Consortia:

  • Two or more legal entities
  • Must be registered in:
    • The project country, or
    • Africa, or
    • EU/EFTA countries

Application Process

The IFE uses a two-stage competitive process:

Stage 1: Concept Note Submission

  • Submit via the SmartME platform
  • Deadline: 30 June 2026 (17:00 CEST)
  • Applications are screened and ranked based on:
    • Eligibility
    • KPIs
    • Qualitative criteria

Stage 2: Full Project Proposal

  • Shortlisted applicants are invited to submit detailed proposals
  • Includes:
    • Due diligence review
    • Possible site visits
  • Successful applicants receive a grant agreement

Important Dates

  • Application Opens: 15 April 2026
  • Application Deadline: 30 June 2026 (17:00 CEST)
  • Questions Deadline: 15 June 2026

Support and Resources

Applicants can access:

  • Guidelines for Applicants (Download Center)
  • FAQ Section
  • Helpdesk: helpdesk-ife@invest-for-jobs.com
  • Email inquiries: cfp-ife.2026@invest-for-jobs.com

Webinars and Information Sessions

To guide applicants, IFE is hosting multiple webinars in English and French, covering:

  • Program objectives
  • Application requirements
  • Platform walkthroughs

Sessions run from April to June 2026, with dedicated presentations for different countries and languages.

Why You Should Apply

This is a high-impact funding opportunity for organizations looking to:

  • Scale their business operations
  • Create sustainable employment
  • Contribute to economic development
  • Access international funding and partnerships

For more information about this opportunity, click here.

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY Here.

The post IFE Call for Proposals 2026: Funding Opportunity for Job-Creating Projects in Côte d’Ivoire, Egypt, and Morocco appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Griots Fellowship 2026 for African Storytellers: Apply Now for the LéO Africa Institute and KAS Uganda Program
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The LéO Africa Institute, in partnership with Konrad-Adenauer-Stiftung (KAS) Uganda, has officially opened applications for the Griots Fellowship 2026, a unique opportunity for African storytellers, creatives, and cultural practitioners to amplify their voices and shape Africa’s narrative. Inspired by the rich West African tradition of the Griot—the keeper of memory, community voice, and cultural identity—the […]

The post Griots Fellowship 2026 for African Storytellers: Apply Now for the LéO Africa Institute and KAS Uganda Program appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The LéO Africa Institute, in partnership with Konrad-Adenauer-Stiftung (KAS) Uganda, has officially opened applications for the Griots Fellowship 2026, a unique opportunity for African storytellers, creatives, and cultural practitioners to amplify their voices and shape Africa’s narrative.

Inspired by the rich West African tradition of the Griot—the keeper of memory, community voice, and cultural identity—the fellowship is designed for individuals who believe storytelling is a powerful tool for reclaiming Africa’s place in the global conversation.

Now entering its second cohort, the Griots Fellowship is seeking practitioners with compelling stories to tell, meaningful projects to develop, and the courage to share their voices with the world.

What is the Griots Fellowship?

The Griots Fellowship is a leadership and storytelling development program created for writers, filmmakers, podcasters, musicians, visual artists, oral historians, and other cultural practitioners across Africa.

The fellowship combines storytelling, leadership, media ethics, and public speaking to help fellows refine their craft and build lasting impact through their narratives.

Participants will engage in a transformative journey that culminates in delivering a powerful TED-style presentation known as The LéO Africa Talk before a live audience.

2026 Cohort Theme

“Stories that will Define Our Future”

This year’s theme focuses on the narratives that will shape Africa’s next chapter—stories of identity, resilience, innovation, leadership, heritage, and transformation.

Fellows will explore how their personal and community experiences can contribute to defining the continent’s future.

Fellowship Program Areas

Selected fellows will go through four key stages of development:

1. Narrative Strategy & Craft

Participants will sharpen their storytelling skills, strengthen their message, and refine the purpose behind their work.

2. Media Production Ethics

The fellowship explores responsibility, representation, and integrity in storytelling, helping fellows understand the ethical dimensions of media creation.

3. Platform Building

Fellows will learn how to expand their visibility, build audiences, and create platforms that give their work greater reach and influence.

4. The LéO Africa Talk

Each fellow will prepare and deliver a carefully crafted TED-style talk before a live audience, showcasing their story and perspective.

Who Should Apply?

Applications are open to:

  • Emerging leaders aged 25 to 45
  • Individuals based anywhere on the African continent
  • Writers, artists, filmmakers, podcasters, musicians, oral historians, and cultural practitioners
  • Storytellers with a personal or community-rooted story they are committed to bringing to life

Whether your work is in literature, film, visual arts, music, oral storytelling, or digital media, this fellowship offers a powerful platform for your voice to be heard.

Why You Should Apply

The Griots Fellowship offers more than training—it provides a space for reflection, visibility, and influence.

Benefits include:

  • Professional storytelling and leadership development
  • Mentorship and guidance from experienced practitioners
  • Public speaking and presentation training
  • Increased visibility through The LéO Africa Talk
  • A network of changemakers and storytellers across Africa
  • A chance to shape the narratives that define the continent’s future

How to Apply

Interested applicants can submit their applications through the official application link below:

Apply Here

Deadline is 30th April 2026

Africa’s future will be shaped by the stories its people choose to tell.

The Griots Fellowship 2026 is an invitation for bold storytellers to step forward, own their narratives, and influence the continent’s cultural and intellectual future.

If you have a story that deserves to be heard, this is your opportunity.

Discover similar opportunities Here.

The post Griots Fellowship 2026 for African Storytellers: Apply Now for the LéO Africa Institute and KAS Uganda Program appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Tony Elumelu Foundation Careers 2026: Brand Officer, Research Manager & M&E Officer Roles in Lagos, Nigeria
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Tony Elumelu Foundation (TEF), Africa’s leading philanthropy dedicated to empowering entrepreneurs, is currently recruiting for multiple strategic roles in Lagos, Nigeria. These positions—Brand Officer, Research Manager, and Monitoring & Evaluation (M&E) Officer—offer professionals the opportunity to contribute directly to Africa’s economic transformation through entrepreneurship, research, and impact-driven initiatives. Founded in 2010 by African investor […]

The post Tony Elumelu Foundation Careers 2026: Brand Officer, Research Manager & M&E Officer Roles in Lagos, Nigeria appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Tony Elumelu Foundation (TEF), Africa’s leading philanthropy dedicated to empowering entrepreneurs, is currently recruiting for multiple strategic roles in Lagos, Nigeria. These positions—Brand Officer, Research Manager, and Monitoring & Evaluation (M&E) Officer—offer professionals the opportunity to contribute directly to Africa’s economic transformation through entrepreneurship, research, and impact-driven initiatives.

Founded in 2010 by African investor and philanthropist Tony O. Elumelu, TEF has committed over $100 million to empowering African entrepreneurs. Through its flagship programmes, partnerships, and digital platforms such as TEFConnect, the Foundation has supported over 9,000 entrepreneurs across 54 African countries and reached over 1 million individuals.

These career opportunities are ideal for professionals passionate about development, entrepreneurship, policy, research, and strategic communications.

About the Tony Elumelu Foundation (TEF)

The Tony Elumelu Foundation is a pan-African organization focused on:

  • Driving entrepreneurship across Africa
  • Promoting private-sector-led economic growth
  • Reducing poverty and unemployment
  • Supporting innovation and inclusive development

Through partnerships with global organizations such as the EU, UNDP, and the Red Cross, TEF continues to expand its impact and strengthen Africa’s entrepreneurial ecosystem.

Available Job Positions

1. Brand Officer (Lagos, Nigeria)

Job Purpose

The Brand Officer plays a critical role in shaping and maintaining TEF’s brand identity across all platforms. This role ensures that the Foundation’s messaging, storytelling, and visual communication reflect its mission and reinforce its leadership in African entrepreneurship.

Key Responsibilities

Brand Management & Strategy

  • Execute TEF’s brand strategy across communication channels
  • Maintain brand consistency across all materials
  • Protect and enhance brand reputation
  • Ensure alignment with TEF’s identity and values

Marketing, Communications & Storytelling

  • Develop brand-led campaigns and content
  • Support programme launches and communication initiatives
  • Create reports, presentations, and digital assets
  • Amplify impact stories across Africa

Graphic Design & Visual Content

  • Design digital and print materials (social media, reports, flyers)
  • Translate brand strategy into compelling visuals
  • Maintain visual consistency across platforms

Digital & Media Support

  • Manage brand presence across digital platforms
  • Monitor media coverage and brand performance
  • Support reporting and analytics

Stakeholder Engagement

  • Collaborate with internal teams and external partners
  • Coordinate with agencies and vendors
  • Promote internal brand awareness

Qualifications & Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Branding, or related field
  • Minimum of 5 years’ experience
  • Strong storytelling and branding expertise
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
  • Proficiency in digital and social media platforms

Learn More Here

2. Research Manager – Africapitalism Institute (Lagos, Nigeria)

Job Purpose

The Research Manager will lead the revival and strategic direction of the Africapitalism Institute, positioning it as a leading research and thought leadership platform across Africa.

Key Responsibilities

Strategic Leadership

  • Lead the Africapitalism Institute
  • Develop long-term research agendas
  • Build partnerships with universities and institutions

Research Design & Execution

  • Conduct high-quality research on entrepreneurship and economic development
  • Design quantitative and qualitative methodologies

Data Analysis

  • Analyze large datasets using econometric and statistical tools
  • Generate insights for policymakers and stakeholders

Thought Leadership

  • Produce policy papers, research reports, and publications
  • Support global academic and policy engagement

Stakeholder Engagement

  • Collaborate with governments, think tanks, and development partners
  • Drive Africapitalism discourse globally

Qualifications & Requirements

  • Master’s degree (PhD preferred)
  • Minimum of 10 years’ research experience
  • Strong publication record
  • Advanced data analysis skills (Stata, SPSS, etc.)
  • Experience with policy engagement and academic research

Learn More Here

3. Monitoring & Evaluation (M&E) Officer (Lagos, Nigeria)

Job Purpose

The M&E Officer will support the design and implementation of monitoring systems to evaluate TEF’s programmes across Africa, ensuring effective impact measurement and learning.

Key Responsibilities

Monitoring & Evaluation Systems

  • Develop and implement M&E frameworks across 54 countries
  • Conduct impact assessments and data collection

Data Management & Reporting

  • Maintain centralized data systems
  • Conduct quarterly data quality assessments

Learning & Knowledge Sharing

  • Capture lessons learned and best practices
  • Develop case studies and impact stories

Collaboration

  • Work with communications and programme teams
  • Support advocacy and campaign strategies

Qualifications & Requirements

  • Master’s degree
  • Minimum of 5 years’ experience in M&E
  • Strong data analysis and reporting skills
  • Experience with tools such as Excel, SPSS, BI tools
  • Ability to work in multicultural environments

Learn More Here

Core Competencies Across Roles

  • Strong alignment with TEF’s mission and values
  • Passion for entrepreneurship and development
  • High attention to detail
  • Ability to work in fast-paced, multicultural environments
  • Strong collaboration and accountability

Why Work at the Tony Elumelu Foundation?

Working at TEF offers:

  • Opportunity to impact millions of entrepreneurs across Africa
  • Exposure to international partnerships and development programs
  • Career growth in a high-impact, mission-driven organization
  • Participation in one of Africa’s largest entrepreneurial ecosystems
  • Access to global networks in development, policy, and research

Location

All roles are based in:

  • Lagos, Nigeria

Application Details

  • Application deadlines for all three positions is April 30th, 2026.
  • Candidates should ensure all required qualifications and experience are met before applying
  • Candidates are to send all application materials to careers@heirsholdings.com before the deadline.

Conclusion

The Tony Elumelu Foundation’s 2026 recruitment presents a unique opportunity for professionals to contribute to Africa’s transformation through entrepreneurship, research, and development impact.

Whether you are a branding expert, a seasoned researcher, or an M&E specialist, these roles provide a platform to drive meaningful change at scale across the continent.

Click HERE to discover more global Job opportunities on OFY.

The post Tony Elumelu Foundation Careers 2026: Brand Officer, Research Manager & M&E Officer Roles in Lagos, Nigeria appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AmaliTech National Service Programme 2026/2027 | AmaliTech | AI-Powered Technology National Service Opportunity in Ghana
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The AmaliTech National Service Programme 2026/2027 is a structured, technology-focused national service opportunity designed for Ghanaian graduates who are eligible for the 2026/2027 National Service year and are interested in building a practical, AI-powered tech career. This National Service Programme combines hands-on industry experience, mentorship, and exposure to international project teams, offering participants a strong […]

The post AmaliTech National Service Programme 2026/2027 | AmaliTech | AI-Powered Technology National Service Opportunity in Ghana appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The AmaliTech National Service Programme 2026/2027 is a structured, technology-focused national service opportunity designed for Ghanaian graduates who are eligible for the 2026/2027 National Service year and are interested in building a practical, AI-powered tech career. This National Service Programme combines hands-on industry experience, mentorship, and exposure to international project teams, offering participants a strong foundation for long-term careers in software, data, and digital transformation roles.

Organised by AmaliTech, a leading technology services company with a strong focus on talent development in Africa, the AmaliTech National Service Programme goes beyond traditional National Service placements. The programme integrates real-world project work, structured learning pathways, and professional development within an AI-enabled environment that prepares participants for employment beyond the National Service Scheme (NSS).

Overview of the AmaliTech National Service Programme 2026/2027

The AmaliTech National Service Programme is tailored for graduates who want to apply their academic knowledge in real technology environments while serving their mandatory national service. Participants are trained and deployed across specialised technology pathways, supported by mentors, modern tools, and structured assessments.

The programme places strong emphasis on software development, digital transformation, data engineering, and systems engineering, ensuring that participants graduate from the programme with both technical expertise and workplace readiness.

Specialisation Pathways

Participants in the AmaliTech National Service Programme 2026/2027 are placed into one of the following specialisation pathways, depending on their background, interest, and assessment outcomes:

Digital Transformation

This pathway focuses on building cross-functional business and technology skills. Participants develop competencies in product ownership, project management, consulting, and business analysis. The goal is to prepare graduates to work closely with business leaders to drive value through technology-enabled solutions.

Experience Engineering

The Experience Engineering pathway is centred on designing and delivering high-quality digital products. It combines design thinking with technical skills in UI/UX design, frontend development, mobile application development, and quality assurance. Participants also leverage modern AI tools to build, test, and validate user-centred digital solutions.

Systems Engineering

This pathway equips participants with strong architectural foundations and specialised technical skills in backend development, cloud computing, cybersecurity, DevOps, and test automation engineering. Learners are trained to design secure and scalable systems, orchestrate Agentic AI solutions, and protect mission-critical platforms.

Data Engineering & Analytics

The Data Engineering & Analytics pathway provides end-to-end exposure to data platforms. Participants gain skills in data engineering, business intelligence, and machine learning, enabling them to support responsible, AI-driven insights and data-informed decision-making across organisations.

Eligibility Criteria

Must-Have Requirements

Applicants must meet all of the following criteria to be eligible:

  • Must be eligible for National Service for the 2026/2027 service year
  • Must have a major or minor in:
    • Computer Science
    • Computer Engineering
    • Information Technology (IT)
    • Management Information Systems (MIS)
    • Business Informatics
  • Must have some experience in IT consulting, software engineering, or data engineering
  • Must demonstrate a strong passion for a career in IT consulting, software engineering, or data engineering

Exceptional Applicants

Applicants from other educational backgrounds may also apply if they possess relevant professional experience or recognised certifications related to technology, software, or data fields.

The Hecker Fellowship (Top Performer Track)

Outstanding participants in the AmaliTech National Service Programme may be selected for the Hecker Fellowship, an exclusive track designed for top-performing NSS trainees. The fellowship accelerates career growth and provides enhanced leadership exposure.

Benefits of the Hecker Fellowship include:

  • Direct mentorship from the CEO
  • Sign-on bonus
  • Executive-level exposure and career acceleration opportunities

Why Join AmaliTech for National Service?

Full-Time Employment Opportunities

Participants who excel during the programme may be offered full-time junior engineer positions at AmaliTech, with competitive compensation and opportunities for rapid career advancement.

Real-World Industry Experience

Participants work on international project teams, gaining exposure to global standards, tools, and collaborative workflows.

Financial and Technical Support

The programme provides:

  • A competitive monthly stipend
  • A complete workstation, including a laptop, monitor, and headphones
  • High-speed internet access and required software licenses

Career Guidance and Professional Development

Participants benefit from structured career guidance, networking opportunities, and training in cross-cultural communication, interview preparation, and workplace effectiveness.

Company Culture

The programme includes social activities, team-building events, and end-of-year bonus packages that promote a positive and supportive company culture.

Application Process

The AmaliTech National Service Programme application process consists of four structured stages:

  1. Application and Aptitude Test
    All applicants complete a multiple-choice assessment covering abstract reasoning, verbal reasoning, and numerical reasoning.
  2. Specialist Assessment
    Candidates complete a technical assessment related to their selected specialisation pathway.
  3. Take-Home Lab and Interviews
    Interviews are based on the lab submission and assess technical depth, leadership potential, communication skills, and collaboration ability.
  4. Offer to Shortlisted Candidates
    Successful candidates receive an offer to complete their National Service with AmaliTech in Accra, Kumasi, or Takoradi.

Important Dates

  • Service Year: 2026/2027 National Service period
  • Application timelines and assessment stages follow the official AmaliTech recruitment schedule via the application portal

Applicants are encouraged to apply early and carefully follow all instructions provided on the official website.

How to Apply

Interested and qualified applicants should complete the online application by clicking the link below and following all instructions provided on the application portal.

APPLY HERE

 

The AmaliTech National Service Programme 2026/2027 offers an exceptional opportunity for Ghanaian graduates to complete their National Service while gaining practical, AI-powered technology experience, professional mentorship, and exposure to international projects. With structured specialisation pathways, strong career support, and potential full-time employment opportunities, this National Service Programme stands out as a strategic pathway into long-term careers in software, data, and digital transformation.

For more opportunities visit Opportunities for youth

The post AmaliTech National Service Programme 2026/2027 | AmaliTech | AI-Powered Technology National Service Opportunity in Ghana appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Sierra Ride Electric Vehicle Initiative 2026: Apply for UNDP and Sierra Leone Youth Driver EV Programme
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Ministry of Youth Affairs in Sierra Leone, in collaboration with the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), has officially opened applications for the “Sierra Ride” Electric Vehicle Initiative 2026, a pilot programme designed to modernize the country’s commercial transport sector while creating new economic opportunities for young drivers. This innovative project introduces electric vehicles (EVs) […]

The post Sierra Ride Electric Vehicle Initiative 2026: Apply for UNDP and Sierra Leone Youth Driver EV Programme appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Ministry of Youth Affairs in Sierra Leone, in collaboration with the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), has officially opened applications for the “Sierra Ride” Electric Vehicle Initiative 2026, a pilot programme designed to modernize the country’s commercial transport sector while creating new economic opportunities for young drivers.

This innovative project introduces electric vehicles (EVs) into Sierra Leone’s transport system to reduce carbon emissions, improve urban mobility, and promote green jobs for youth across the country.

About the Sierra Ride Initiative

The Sierra Ride Initiative is part of Sierra Leone’s broader national strategy to transform urban transportation through sustainable and climate-friendly mobility solutions.

The programme aims to replace traditional fuel-powered commercial vehicles—especially the widely used three-wheelers known locally as kekes—with environmentally friendly electric alternatives.

Traditional kekes consume significant amounts of gasoline and contribute heavily to urban air pollution. By transitioning to electric vehicles, Sierra Leone hopes to:

  • Reduce greenhouse gas emissions
  • Improve urban air quality
  • Lower transport operating costs
  • Promote road safety
  • Support climate-smart innovation
  • Create sustainable youth employment opportunities

The initiative is co-financed and implemented by the Government of Sierra Leone with support from:

  • United Nations Development Programme (UNDP)
  • United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP)
  • Global Environment Facility (GEF)
  • European Union through the SOLUTIONSPlus Project
  • Local transport and environmental stakeholders

Who Can Apply?

The programme is specifically targeting young Sierra Leoneans aged 21 to 35 years who are interested in becoming part of the country’s growing green transport sector.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Must be between 21 and 35 years old
  • Have at least 3 years of driving experience
  • Possess a valid driver’s licence
  • Have a clean driving record
  • Must have completed Senior Secondary Education
  • Provide a Police Clearance Certificate
  • Show willingness to undergo training on electric vehicle technology

Special Inclusion Priority

The Ministry of Youth Affairs strongly encourages applications from:

  • Women
  • Persons with disabilities

This aligns with the government’s commitment to promoting gender equality and inclusive economic empowerment in the transport industry.

Why Sierra Ride Matters

During the 2025 International Youth Day celebrations, Minister of Youth Affairs Ibrahim Sannoh announced a youth empowerment package that included the rollout of 50 electric vehicles, with special priority for women drivers.

The minister emphasized that promoting climate technology, innovation, and electric mobility is a major national priority.

This initiative also builds on UNDP’s recent efforts to empower young women with technical and vocational skills. Earlier in 2026, UNDP partnered with NAYCOM and AFTEC to certify 250 young women in fields such as:

  • Auto mechanics
  • Renewable energy systems
  • Technical vocational skills

The Sierra Ride project now creates a direct pathway for young people—especially women—to apply these skills in real-world income-generating opportunities.

Benefits of the Programme

Selected participants will benefit from:

  • Access to electric commercial vehicles
  • Training on electric vehicle operations and maintenance
  • Entry into the growing green transport economy
  • Reduced fuel and operating costs
  • Opportunities for sustainable income generation
  • Participation in Sierra Leone’s climate-smart mobility transition

How to Apply

Interested applicants should submit the following documents:

  • Updated CV
  • Cover Letter
  • Copy of Driver’s Licence

Submission Options

Applications can be submitted via:

Email

info@moya.gov.sl

Physical Submission

Ministry of Youth Affairs
24 Scan Drive
Wilberforce, Freetown
Sierra Leone

Application Deadline

Deadline: 8th May 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early to avoid last-minute delays.

Visit Here, for more details.

The Sierra Ride Electric Vehicle Initiative 2026 offers more than just a driving opportunity—it is a gateway into Sierra Leone’s green economy and a bold step toward sustainable urban transportation.

For young drivers seeking employment, empowerment, and the chance to be part of a climate-friendly future, this programme is an excellent opportunity to accelerate both career growth and national development.

If you meet the eligibility criteria, this is the perfect time to take the wheel and drive toward a greener future.

Discover similar opportunities Here

The post Sierra Ride Electric Vehicle Initiative 2026: Apply for UNDP and Sierra Leone Youth Driver EV Programme appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Canada LIFTs Program 2026: Transformative Funding & Learning Opportunity for Small and Medium-Sized Organizations
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The Canada LIFTs Program is an innovative and purpose-driven initiative designed to empower small and medium-sized organizations (SMOs) based in Canada and their global partners. This program goes beyond traditional funding models by combining financial support with capacity-building resources, collaborative learning, and meaningful peer engagement. At its core, Canada LIFTs seeks to reshape international cooperation […]

The post Canada LIFTs Program 2026: Transformative Funding & Learning Opportunity for Small and Medium-Sized Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Canada LIFTs Program is an innovative and purpose-driven initiative designed to empower small and medium-sized organizations (SMOs) based in Canada and their global partners. This program goes beyond traditional funding models by combining financial support with capacity-building resources, collaborative learning, and meaningful peer engagement.

At its core, Canada LIFTs seeks to reshape international cooperation by reducing systemic barriers, redistributing power, and strengthening the broader development ecosystem in which SMOs operate.

What is the Canada LIFTs Program?

Canada LIFTs is a comprehensive funding and learning platform aimed at supporting community-led international development initiatives. It recognizes the critical role that smaller organizations play in driving impactful, grassroots change and provides them with the tools and resources needed to scale their efforts effectively.

Key Objectives of Canada LIFTs

The program is structured around several transformative goals:

  1. Reduce Barriers to Access
    • Simplify funding processes for smaller organizations
    • Provide equitable opportunities for underrepresented groups
  2. Shift Power Dynamics
    • Promote locally led development initiatives
    • Encourage decision-making at the community level
  3. Strengthen Organizational Ecosystems
    • Build long-term sustainability for SMOs
    • Foster collaboration across organizations and regions

Program Components

Canada LIFTs stands out due to its integrated approach, combining multiple forms of support:

  • Funding Opportunities
    • Direct financial support through dedicated funding streams
  • Learning Resources
    • Access to tools, guides, and training for organizational growth
  • Peer Networking
    • Opportunities to connect with like-minded organizations and partners
  • Capacity Building
    • Strengthening leadership, strategy, and operational capabilities

Available Funding Streams

Applicants can register for one of the following funds:

  • Canada LIFTs Momentum Fund
    • Designed to accelerate existing initiatives and scale impact
  • Canada LIFTs Together Fund
    • Focused on fostering partnerships and collaborative projects

Application Details

  • Application Period: April 1 – June 30, 2026
  • Deadline: June 30, 2026, at 1:00 PM Central Time
  • Registration Requirement: Applicants must register within the application window to be considered

Why You Should Apply

Participating in Canada LIFTs offers numerous advantages:

  • Access to flexible and purpose-driven funding
  • Opportunities to collaborate with global partners
  • Enhanced organizational skills and strategic capacity
  • Inclusion in a supportive and innovative development network
  • Ability to amplify community-led impact on an international scale

Who Should Consider This Program?

Canada LIFTs is particularly beneficial for organizations that:

  • Are small to medium-sized and based in Canada
  • Work in international development or community-led initiatives
  • Seek to expand their reach and effectiveness
  • Value collaboration, innovation, and equity in development work

How to Get Started

Interested organizations should prepare to register for either the Momentum Fund or the Together Fund before the deadline. Early preparation is encouraged to ensure eligibility and a strong application. For more information about this opportunity, click here.

Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here.

The post Canada LIFTs Program 2026: Transformative Funding & Learning Opportunity for Small and Medium-Sized Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: ClimateLaunchpad 2026 Start-up Competition for Climate Tech and Green Innovation (Global Opportunity)
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Are you building a climate-focused startup or have a powerful green business idea that could help shape a more sustainable future? Applications for ClimateLaunchpad 2026 are now open, offering early-stage entrepreneurs the chance to access expert mentorship, investor networks, global exposure, and the opportunity to compete on the international stage. Since 2014, ClimateLaunchpad has grown […]

The post Apply Now: ClimateLaunchpad 2026 Start-up Competition for Climate Tech and Green Innovation (Global Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you building a climate-focused startup or have a powerful green business idea that could help shape a more sustainable future? Applications for ClimateLaunchpad 2026 are now open, offering early-stage entrepreneurs the chance to access expert mentorship, investor networks, global exposure, and the opportunity to compete on the international stage.

Since 2014, ClimateLaunchpad has grown into the world’s largest green business ideas competition, operating across 97 countries and helping more than 5,000 teams transform climate innovations into impactful ventures.

Best of all, the programme is completely free to join, with no participation fees.

About ClimateLaunchpad

ClimateLaunchpad is more than just a competition—it is a global platform dedicated to helping entrepreneurs build solutions for climate change and sustainability challenges.

The programme supports innovators by providing the expertise, validation, and business development skills needed to turn early-stage climate ideas into scalable businesses.

Over the years, ClimateLaunchpad has delivered:

  • More than 500 training bootcamps
  • A strong international network of investors and partners
  • Over €500 million raised in investment by alumni startups

The initiative continues to empower founders who are creating practical solutions for environmental sustainability, clean energy, circular economy, food systems, water management, and other climate-related sectors.

What’s in It for You?

Selected participants in the 2026 ClimateLaunchpad cohort will get the opportunity to:

1. Receive Expert Coaching and Mentorship

Work with experienced coaches and mentors who will help refine your idea and strengthen your business strategy.

2. Validate Your Business Model

Join a structured programme designed to test assumptions, improve your market fit, and sharpen your startup model.

3. Connect with Global Investors

Gain access to a powerful international network of investors, industry experts, and fellow changemakers.

4. Pitch at the Global Grand Final in Singapore

Top-performing startups will advance to the Global Grand Final in Singapore, where they will pitch live and compete for major prizes and recognition.

ClimateLaunchpad 2026 Programme Structure

The programme follows a step-by-step development journey:

Step 0 – Start Learning: Mini-Course

Participants begin with foundational learning resources to prepare for the programme.

Step 1 – Build Your Idea: Boot Camp

A practical training bootcamp focused on developing and strengthening your climate startup idea.

Step 2 – Test and Refine: Follow-Up Coaching and National Finals

Receive additional support while preparing for national-level pitching competitions.

Step 3 – Compete Internationally: Regional Finals

Top startups move forward to regional competitions.

Step 4 – The Global Stage: Global Grand Final

The best teams compete globally at the final international event in Singapore.

Eligibility Requirements

To apply for ClimateLaunchpad 2026, your sustainability venture must meet the following conditions:

  • Your startup must be less than two years old
  • You must have raised less than €200,000 in total funding
  • You must not yet have sold products or services on commercial terms

If your startup meets these criteria, you are eligible to apply.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 15 May 2026 (23:59 CET)

Please note that deadlines may vary depending on your country, so applicants are strongly encouraged to check their local application timeline on the official website.

How to Apply

Interested founders should:

  1. Visit the official ClimateLaunchpad website for country-specific details
  2. Confirm local deadlines and programme information
  3. Submit their application through the official application portal

 

ClimateLaunchpad 2026 is an incredible opportunity for early-stage climate entrepreneurs who want to transform innovative ideas into real-world solutions.

If you are passionate about sustainability and ready to scale your climate startup, this global competition could provide the mentorship, visibility, and funding opportunities needed to take your venture to the next level.

With expert guidance, investor access, and a pathway to the Global Grand Final in Singapore, this is one opportunity green innovators should not miss.

Discover similar Grant opportunity Here.

The post Apply Now: ClimateLaunchpad 2026 Start-up Competition for Climate Tech and Green Innovation (Global Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: WISE Internship 2026 in Europe: Fully Funded Western Balkans Internship Scheme for Young Professionals (Apply by May 4)
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The Young Professionals Network (YPN), with support from the German Foreign Office, has launched an exciting and transformative opportunity for emerging leaders through the Western Balkans Young Professionals Internship Scheme for Empowerment (WISE) 2026. This fully funded internship program is designed to empower young professionals across the Western Balkans by equipping them with practical experience, […]

The post WISE Internship 2026 in Europe: Fully Funded Western Balkans Internship Scheme for Young Professionals (Apply by May 4) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Young Professionals Network (YPN), with support from the German Foreign Office, has launched an exciting and transformative opportunity for emerging leaders through the Western Balkans Young Professionals Internship Scheme for Empowerment (WISE) 2026.

This fully funded internship program is designed to empower young professionals across the Western Balkans by equipping them with practical experience, cross-border exposure, and critical leadership skills needed to thrive in today’s global workforce.

If you are looking to gain international work experience, build a strong professional network, and contribute to regional development, this opportunity is tailored for you.

Application Deadline

The application period runs from April 16 to May 4, 2026.

What is the WISE Internship Scheme?

The WISE Internship Scheme is a first-of-its-kind regional initiative that aims to address:

  • Youth unemployment
  • Skills gaps in the labor market
  • Limited workforce mobility
  • Weak regional economic cooperation

It aligns with major regional frameworks such as:

  • The Common Regional Market
  • The Berlin Process
  • The EU Single Market

Through this program, selected participants will engage in three-month internships across the Western Balkans, gaining hands-on experience while contributing to innovation and collaboration across borders.

Program Overview

  • Duration: 3 months
  • Participants: 12 selected candidates
  • Countries Covered: 6 Western Balkans countries
  • Fields:
    • Technology
    • Communication
    • Green jobs
    • Civic engagement

Key Objectives of WISE

The program is structured around five core pillars:

1. Networking

  • Build a strong network of young professionals
  • Foster collaboration across borders

2. Mobility

  • Enable participants to live and work in a different country within the region
  • Enhance adaptability and global exposure

3. Cooperation

  • Strengthen economic ties between businesses and institutions
  • Promote regional integration

4. Skills Development

  • Align participants’ skills with labor market demands
  • Improve employability

5. Innovation

  • Encourage creative solutions to regional challenges
  • Support broader development agendas

Why You Should Apply for WISE 2026

1. Hands-On Work Experience

  • Work with leading businesses and NGOs
  • Gain real-world exposure in high-demand sectors

2. Professional Skills Training

  • Participate in a 4-day intensive training program in Albania
  • Focus areas:
    • Leadership
    • Workplace ethics
    • Intercultural communication

3. International Mobility

  • Live and work in a different Western Balkans country
  • Experience diverse cultures and work environments

4. Strong Networking Opportunities

  • Connect with professionals, mentors, and peers
  • Build long-term career relationships

5. Fully Funded Internship

  • Monthly stipends provided
  • Travel and accommodation costs covered

6. Certification

  • Receive a professional certificate upon completion

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the WISE Internship 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements:

1. Age Requirement

  • Must be between 20 and 30 years old

2. Educational Background

  • Hold at least a Bachelor’s degree
  • Final-year students or recent graduates may apply with proof of completion

3. Nationality

  • Must be a citizen of one of the Western Balkans countries:
    • Albania
    • Bosnia and Herzegovina
    • Kosovo
    • Montenegro
    • North Macedonia
    • Serbia

4. Experience

  • Minimum of two years of professional experience or:
    • Internship experience
    • Community engagement
    • Leadership involvement

5. Language Requirement

  • Must be fluent in English

6. Personal Qualities

  • Strong motivation and integrity
  • Ability to work independently
  • Openness to learning and collaboration

7. Personal Statement

  • Must demonstrate alignment with WISE goals
  • Show interest in regional cooperation and development

Application Process: Step-by-Step Guide

Step 1: Submit Application

Applicants must provide:

  • CV
  • Recommendation letter
  • Academic credentials
  • Personal statement

Step 2: Screening Phase (May 5 – May 10, 2026)

  • Applications are reviewed to ensure eligibility

Step 3: Interview Stage (May 11 – May 13, 2026)

  • Shortlisted candidates are invited for interviews
  • Focus on:
    • Skills
    • Motivation
    • Career goals

Step 4: Matching Process (May 2026)

  • Candidates are paired with host organizations
  • Matching is based on:
    • Skills
    • Interests
    • Organizational needs

Step 5: Final Selection & Notification

  • Selected applicants receive confirmation and next steps

Important Note

  • The use of AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT) is strictly prohibited during the application process
  • All submissions must be original
  • Any form of plagiarism will lead to disqualification

Program Journey Beyond Selection

Selected participants will go through:

  • Pre-departure orientation
  • Internship placement
  • Ongoing mentorship and support
  • Networking and collaboration activities
  • Final certification upon completion

How to Apply

Interested candidates should apply through the official WISE application portal.

For inquiries, contact:

  • network@ypn.al

APPLY HERE

Final Thoughts

The WISE Internship Scheme 2026 is more than just an internship—it is a career-launching platform designed to shape the next generation of leaders in the Western Balkans.

With full funding, international exposure, and structured development, this program offers a rare opportunity to gain both professional and personal growth while contributing to regional progress.

For more information on this INTERNSHIP VISIT HERE  

For more internship offers, visit OFY HERE

The post WISE Internship 2026 in Europe: Fully Funded Western Balkans Internship Scheme for Young Professionals (Apply by May 4) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for FTPF Orchard Donation 2026: Get Free Fruit Trees for Schools, Nonprofits and Communities
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Communities across the United States now have an incredible opportunity to receive free fruit trees and orchard support through the Fruit Tree Planting Foundation (FTPF). This award-winning international nonprofit is inviting eligible organizations to apply for orchard donations that help fight hunger, improve the environment, and strengthen local communities. If your school, nonprofit, public park, […]

The post Apply for FTPF Orchard Donation 2026: Get Free Fruit Trees for Schools, Nonprofits and Communities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Communities across the United States now have an incredible opportunity to receive free fruit trees and orchard support through the Fruit Tree Planting Foundation (FTPF). This award-winning international nonprofit is inviting eligible organizations to apply for orchard donations that help fight hunger, improve the environment, and strengthen local communities.

If your school, nonprofit, public park, or community garden is looking to create a lasting source of fresh fruit and environmental education, this program could be the perfect fit.

About the Fruit Tree Planting Foundation (FTPF)

The Fruit Tree Planting Foundation (FTPF) is a globally recognized nonprofit charity dedicated to planting fruitful trees and plants to address some of the world’s biggest challenges. Their mission includes:

  • Alleviating world hunger
  • Combating global warming
  • Strengthening communities
  • Improving air quality
  • Enhancing soil and water health

FTPF strategically donates orchards where the harvest can serve people for generations. Their orchards are commonly planted in:

  • Community gardens
  • Public schools
  • City and state parks
  • Low-income neighborhoods
  • Native American reservations
  • Hunger relief sites
  • Animal sanctuaries

The goal is to ensure that fresh fruit remains accessible while creating educational and environmental benefits for the community.

What FTPF Provides

Organizations selected for an orchard donation receive far more than just trees. FTPF offers a complete orchard support package, including:

  • High-quality fruit trees and shrubs
  • Planting equipment
  • On-site orchard design expertise
  • Professional oversight during planting
  • Horticultural workshops
  • Aftercare training and manuals
  • Subsidized deer fencing where needed
  • Drip irrigation support when necessary
  • Volunteer coordination assistance
  • Educational planting-day experiences

For schools, FTPF also provides fun, age-appropriate environmental education programs that teach students about:

  • The importance of trees
  • Healthy fruit consumption
  • Sustainability and environmental stewardship

This makes the orchard project both practical and educational.

Who Can Apply?

FTPF orchard donations are available to organizations that can demonstrate a strong commitment to caring for the trees and using the orchard for charitable purposes.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Nonprofits
  • NGOs
  • Public schools
  • Government entities

Important Note for International Applicants

At the moment, FTPF is not accepting applications from outside the United States because their international programming is currently at capacity.

Applications submitted from outside the U.S. will not be processed at this time.

FTPF Orchard Application Requirements

To qualify, applicants must meet the following requirements:

1. Serve a Charitable Purpose

Applicants must be a nonprofit, NGO, public school, or government entity working toward a charitable mission.

2. Long-Term Commitment to Tree Care

Recipients must commit to maintaining the orchard permanently and using it to support their charitable goals.

3. Suitable Planting Site

Applicants must have enough horticulturally appropriate land to host a grove of fruit trees planted about 15 feet apart.

Most programs require approximately:

  • 15–20 trees minimum

The FTPF application helps assess whether your site is suitable.

4. Land Ownership or Long-Term Access

Applicants must either:

  • Own the planting site
  • Have a long-term lease
  • Partner with a nonprofit or government entity that owns the site with a long-term usage agreement

5. Reliable Irrigation Access

The orchard site must have a dependable year-round water source nearby.

6. Volunteer Participation

Recipients must help coordinate local volunteers for planting day, as community involvement improves long-term orchard stewardship.

Why This Opportunity Matters

An FTPF orchard is more than a tree donation—it is a long-term investment in food security, environmental sustainability, and community empowerment.

Whether you manage a school, a nonprofit, or a public community space, this opportunity can create lasting impact for generations to come.

If your organization is ready to grow fresh food, engage volunteers, and improve your local environment, now is the perfect time to apply for an FTPF orchard.

Application Deadline

There is no fixed deadline for FTPF orchard applications.

Applications are accepted on a rolling basis, meaning organizations can apply at any time.

However, FTPF notes that their schedule is currently near capacity, so response times may be slow. Applicants should be patient, as worthy projects are often considered when suitable opportunities arise.

Once submitted:

  • You will receive an acknowledgment letter
  • Your application remains permanently in FTPF’s database
  • There is no need to reapply if you do not hear back immediately

How to Apply

Interested organizations should:

Step 1: Review the Requirements

Carefully confirm that your organization meets all eligibility criteria.

Step 2: Download the FTPF Application Form

Download the official application form from the FTPF website.

Step 3: Submit via Email

Send your completed application form to:

Email: info@ftpf.org

Discover similar Grant Opportunities Here.

The post Apply for FTPF Orchard Donation 2026: Get Free Fruit Trees for Schools, Nonprofits and Communities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
